summary: A large mixed technical transcript containing repeated live-repository audits, a sharpened rooted-leaf theorem, AQFT and quantum-information arguments, four-dimensional Yang–Mills exposition and several speculative programmes. Stable content was reduced to parent-normalized tree kernels, exact Catalan closure, AQFT nonfactorization/no-signalling, uniform recovery discipline, Gaussian Markov blankets, marginal dimensional analysis and a localized-homotopy boundary identity.

promoted.title: Parent-normalized rooted child kernel
promoted.rationale: The positive parent metric can be divided out locally and recovered exactly through rooted child multiplicities, avoiding a global unpinned sum.

promoted.title: Exact Catalan closure of the rooted tree-shape sum
promoted.rationale: Plane-rooted-tree enumeration gives the exact normalized child-factorial sum and a closed square-root generating function.

promoted.title: Cyclic and separating vacuum excludes product factorization
promoted.rationale: The short proof is formal once the Reeh–Schlieder cyclic/separating hypotheses and nontriviality of the distant algebra are supplied.

promoted.title: Complete local operations preserve distant statistics
promoted.rationale: Commutation with the distant observable and Kraus completeness imply an exact expectation identity.

promoted.title: CMI recovery and the uniform-channel quantifier discipline
promoted.rationale: Finite-dimensional recovery theorems support a single-channel reconstruction statement, while code recovery additionally requires reference-system preservation.

promoted.title: Sparse Gaussian precision gives exact conditional factorization
promoted.rationale: For a positive-definite finite Gaussian, a zero precision block between interior and exterior removes their cross term after conditioning on the collar.

promoted.title: Localized homotopy defect as a projector commutator
promoted.rationale: The ungraded boundary identity follows by direct expansion and identifies the exact term that a later locality estimate must bound.

promoted.title: Four-dimensional marginality of Yang–Mills
promoted.rationale: Canonical power counting yields a dimensionless gauge coupling exactly in four dimensions and explains the marginal RG regime.

promoted.title: Reeh–Schlieder preparation is qualitative, not uniform teleportation
promoted.rationale: Cyclicity gives state-dependent local approximants, but no useful uniform success, energy or operator-norm bound follows.

quarantined.title: Type-III AQFT CMI recovery
quarantined.rationale: The finite-dimensional theorem does not by itself define the required entropy, fidelity or recovery channel for type-III local algebras.

quarantined.title: Everettian branch-controlled recovery
quarantined.rationale: The branch register is interpretive and no branch-independent coherent recovery theorem is supplied.

quarantined.title: Modified hole metric as Schur-complement geometry
quarantined.rationale: Eliminating hole variables can create dense effective boundary couplings, so no metric comparison is established.

quarantined.title: One colored forest for Gaussian and Ursell edges
quarantined.rationale: No coefficient identity proves equivalence with the composed localization, Mayer and Ursell construction while preserving the exact target.

quarantined.title: Variational decay allocation and polymer stopping scale
quarantined.rationale: The optimization depends on an uncomputed moment function and the physical stopping-scale claim needs a calibrated continuum trajectory.

quarantined.title: Boundary commutator as a small Yang–Mills RG activity
quarantined.rationale: The algebraic identity lacks a concrete gauge differential, local homotopy, support proof and polymer-norm estimate.

quarantined.title: Weil prime-layer RG and Schur–Catalan route to RH
quarantined.rationale: The scale matrices, coercivity baseline and summable defects are proposed rather than constructed, and several formulae are only schematic.

quarantined.title: Stieltjes and collar-Schur programme for RH
quarantined.rationale: The claimed equivalence and prime-side positivity require exact canonical-product, meromorphic and sign hypotheses not supplied in the source.

quarantined.title: Exact Mayer–Ward transgression
quarantined.rationale: No nonlinear support-preserving identity and quantitative remainder bound are proved for the target-fiber activity.

discarded.reason: The fixed-tree Appendix-F implementation memo is repeated almost verbatim and substantially overlaps the already curated rooted-leaf material.
Commit hashes, job counts, proved/open inventories and commit sequences belong to a fast-moving external repository and are not stable graph knowledge.
Long introductions to gauge fields, curvature, lattice regularization and the general mass-gap problem duplicate canonical domain nodes and textbooks.
Many-worlds, cabalistic, portal and universal-interconnection language is not mathematical evidence for the promoted claims.
Article rankings, copy-paste prompts, response templates and exact commit choreography are transient workflow material.

Gaussian collar interpolation, lattice-to-physical scaling, Ward weight variation, block coercivity, support-role separation and target-preserving leaf summation were already curated in versions 2.1 and 2.2.

verification_queue.request: Transcribe a primary Reeh–Schlieder statement with the exact region, spectrum and cyclic/separating assumptions used by the nonfactorization proof.
verification_queue.reason: The algebraic derivation is short, but its physical scope depends on the theorem hypotheses.

verification_queue.request: Check the exact models, regions and state classes for maximal Bell violation before promoting a generic AQFT Bell node.
verification_queue.reason: The source text states a broad result whose hypotheses must not be weakened.

verification_queue.request: Fix fidelity conventions, constants and recovery-map dependence from Fawzi–Renner and the universal recovery theorem.
verification_queue.reason: Different fidelity and purified-distance conventions change the displayed bound.

verification_queue.request: Locate a precise split or operator-algebraic recovery theorem before applying finite-dimensional CMI language to type-III AQFT algebras.
verification_queue.reason: The finite tensor-product theorem is not automatically valid in the intended AQFT setting.

verification_queue.request: Compare the exact labelled tree set, fixed root, child-count orientation and normalization with the current repository theorem.
verification_queue.reason: The Catalan equality is formal only after conventions match exactly.

verification_queue.request: Test the conditional-factorization theorem on the actual gauge-fixed, projected or hard-constrained precision operator.
verification_queue.reason: Constraints may introduce a nonlocal effective A-C coupling.

verification_queue.request: Derive the graded projector-commutator identity with fixed degrees and Koszul signs.
verification_queue.reason: Only the ungraded identity is promoted as formal.

verification_queue.request: Continue primary transcription of second-Ursell coefficients, moment constants, shifted metrics, support conventions and the concrete CMP116 activity producer.
verification_queue.reason: The refined finite closure does not supply the physical source activity or its constants.

verification_queue.request: Audit the Weil, Stieltjes, Jensen and De Bruijn–Newman formulae before retaining any arithmetic RG proposal beyond quarantine.
verification_queue.reason: Several statements are schematic and could fail by sign, domain or growth conventions.
